title: Source rows: favicons and activity dots
description: Non-runnable feature container. Coordinates all child beads for adding favicons and activity dots to Sources-tab source rows. Favicons are fetched from DuckDuckGo (icons.duckduckgo.com/ip3/<host>.ico) derived from feed_url, with two-level memory+disk cache. Activity dots mirror the web sidebar macro: blue dot for has_priority, gray dot for has_new, nothing otherwise. The dot fields are decoded defensively so existing API responses (fields absent) show no dots and gain them automatically when the backend ships the fields.
acceptance_criteria: xcodebuild test exits 0 including new Source decoding and favicon URL tests
Each source row shows its favicon leading with a neutral SF Symbol globe placeholder when unavailable — no layout jump
Favicons are cached on disk; scrolling away and back does not re-fetch for the same host
Blue dot shown for has_priority=true, gray dot for has_new=true (not priority), no dot otherwise
On current API (has_new/has_priority absent) no dots appear and no decoding error
Dots carry distinct accessibility labels (not color-only distinction)
Feeds and Categories sections are unchanged
No hardcoded hex colors; Liquid Glass conventions preserved
